message:
[LOW] fcp-crypto: metamorphic tests for Ed25519 context-sign framing

sign_with_context prepends `(context.len() as u64).to_le_bytes()` to the
blake3 digest input. That length prefix is load-bearing — without it,
`(ctx="a", msg="bc")` and `(ctx="ab", msg="c")` would hash identically
and signatures bound to one pair would verify under the other. The
existing tests cover wrong-message and wrong-context rejection but
leave the concat-ambiguity and role-swap invariants uncovered.

Two new metamorphic tests:

- sign_with_context_defeats_concat_ambiguity: constructs the (a,bc) /
  (ab,c) adversarial pair, asserts distinct signatures AND mutual
  cross-verification rejection. A regression that dropped the length
  prefix would flip both assertions.

- sign_with_context_not_symmetric_under_role_swap: a signature bound
  to (ctx=alpha, msg=beta) must not verify under the swapped
  (ctx=beta, msg=alpha) — guards against call-site confusion where
  the two slice arguments get transposed.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib sign_with_context → 6 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
